### Access X and Y Axis Domains in Recharts LineChart with React Hooks

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/hooks/useAxisDomain.mdx

Demonstrates how to use useXAxisDomain and useYAxisDomain hooks within a Recharts LineChart component to retrieve and log axis domain values. The hooks must be called within a chart context component, and they automatically access the chart's axis configuration to return domain information for rendering or debugging purposes.

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { useXAxisDomain, useYAxisDomain, Line, YAxis, XAxis, LineChart } from 'recharts';

const MyComponent = () => {
  const xAxisDomain = useXAxisDomain();
  const yAxisDomain = useYAxisDomain();
  console.log('X Axis Domain:', xAxisDomain);
  console.log('Y Axis Domain:', yAxisDomain);
  return null;
};

const MyChart = () => (
  <LineChart data={data}>
    <XAxis dataKey="name" />
    <YAxis />
    <Line type="monotone" dataKey="value" />
    <MyComponent />
  </LineChart>
);
```

--------------------------------

### Customize Axis Tick Component - Recharts

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/Customise.mdx

Renders custom axis tick labels with rotation transformation. Extracts x, y, stroke, and payload from args and transforms the text with a -35 degree rotation. Enables readable angled axis labels in charts.

```javascript
export const CustomizedAxisTick = (...args)=>{
  const {  x, y, stroke, payload } = args[0];
  return (
    <g transform={`translate(${x},${y})`}>
      <text x={0} y={0} dy={16} textAnchor="end" fill="#666" transform="rotate(-35)">
        {payload.value}
      </text>
    </g>
  );
};
```

--------------------------------

### Render Basic Recharts Bar Chart with Default Axes

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks

This JSX snippet renders a Recharts BarChart displaying solar system data. It uses an `XAxis` for names and a `YAxis` for mass, demonstrating default axis behavior where the Y-axis range automatically covers all data, making smaller values invisible due to the large scale difference.

```jsx
<ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={400}>
  <BarChart data={solarSystem} width={100} height={100}>
    <XAxis dataKey="name" />
    <YAxis width={100} label={{ value: 'Mass [kg]', position: 'insideLeft', dx: 0, dy: 20, angle: -90 }} />
    <Bar dataKey="massKg" unit="kg" />
    <Tooltip />
  </BarChart>
</ResponsiveContainer>
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Custom Domain with Data Overflow for Recharts YAxis

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks

This Recharts YAxis configuration defines a custom numerical domain using `domain={[1e23, 2e27]}`. By setting `allowDataOverflow` to `true`, the axis will strictly adhere to the specified domain, effectively cutting off data points that fall outside this range, such as the Sun's mass in the solar system example.

```jsx
<YAxis
  domain={[1e23, 2e27]}
  allowDataOverflow
/>
```

--------------------------------

### Render Bar Chart with Customized Axis Tick and Label - Recharts React TSX

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/Customise.mdx

Creates a complete bar chart with customized axis ticks rotated -35 degrees and custom bar labels. Uses CustomizedAxisTick component for x-axis and renderCustomBarLabel function for bar value labels. Requires data prop and Recharts library imports.

```javascript
<BarChart width={600} height={300} data={data} margin={{ top: 5, right: 20, left: 10, bottom: 20 }}>
  <XAxis dataKey="name" tick={<CustomizedAxisTick />} />
  <YAxis />
  <Bar dataKey="uv" barSize={30} fill="#8884d8" label={renderCustomBarLabel} />
</BarChart>
```

--------------------------------

### Display Multivariate Data with Radar Chart in Recharts

Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt

Visualize and compare multiple variables across categories using a radar/spider chart format. Displays multivariate data for two entities (Student A and Student B) with filled areas, custom colors, and configurable opacity. Uses PolarGrid, PolarAngleAxis, and PolarRadiusAxis for proper polar coordinate system setup.

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { RadarChart, PolarGrid, PolarAngleAxis, PolarRadiusAxis, Radar, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';

const data = [
  { subject: 'Math', A: 120, B: 110, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'Chinese', A: 98, B: 130, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'English', A: 86, B: 130, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'Geography', A: 99, B: 100, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'Physics', A: 85, B: 90, fullMark: 150 },
  { subject: 'History', A: 65, B: 85, fullMark: 150 }
];

function RadarChartExample() {
  return (
    <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={400}>
      <RadarChart cx="50%" cy="50%" outerRadius="80%" data={data}>
        <PolarGrid />
        <PolarAngleAxis dataKey="subject" />
        <PolarRadiusAxis angle={90} domain={[0, 150]} />
        <Radar
          name="Student A"
          dataKey="A"
          stroke="#8884d8"
          fill="#8884d8"
          fillOpacity={0.6}
        />
        <Radar
          name="Student B"
          dataKey="B"
          stroke="#82ca9d"
          fill="#82ca9d"
          fillOpacity={0.6}
        />
        <Legend />
      </RadarChart>
    </ResponsiveContainer>
  );
}

export default RadarChartExample;
```

--------------------------------

### Format YAxis Ticks with Custom Unit Conversion in Recharts JSX

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks

Demonstrates how to customize Y-axis tick labels in Recharts using the tickFormatter prop. The example converts kilogram values to yottagrams by dividing by 1e24 and formatting to two decimal places. This allows for flexible unit transformations and custom number formatting on chart axes.

```jsx
function kgToYottagram(value: number): string {
  // the data is defined in kg
  const yottagram = value / 1e24;
  return `${yottagram.toFixed(2)}`;
}
// ...
<YAxis
  tickFormatter={kgToYottagram}
/>
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Recharts API Documentation for Components

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/omnidoc/README.md

Utilize the API Documentation Generator to auto-generate API documentation files from TypeScript source code. This tool reads comments and type definitions, overwriting existing `en-US` files in `www/src/docs/api`. You can specify individual components as arguments or generate for all allowlisted components if no arguments are provided.

```shell
npm run omnidoc [component1] [component2] ...
```

```shell
npm run omnidoc
```

```shell
npm run omnidoc CartesianGrid
```

```shell
npm run omnidoc XAxis YAxis ZAxis
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Recharts YAxis as Category Type

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks

This Recharts YAxis configuration sets the `type` prop to 'category'. This treats numerical data as discrete categorical values, displaying each unique mass value as a separate tick rather than a continuous spectrum, which can lead to unsorted and unrounded ticks.

```jsx
<YAxis type='category' label={{ value: 'Mass [kg]', position: 'insideLeft', dx: 0, dy: 20, angle: -90 }} />
```

--------------------------------

### Set Logarithmic Scale and Custom Domain for Recharts YAxis

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks

This Recharts YAxis configuration applies a logarithmic scale using `scale="log"` to better visualize data with a wide range of values, like planetary masses. A custom domain `domain={[1e22, 3e30]}` is also specified, which is often required for the logarithmic scale to function correctly.

```jsx
<YAxis
  scale="log"
  domain={[1e22, 3e30]}
/>
```

--------------------------------

### Define Solar System Data Array for Recharts

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks

This JavaScript array defines the data points for celestial bodies in a solar system, including properties like name, orbital distance, radius, mass, and a fill color. It serves as input for Recharts components to visualize planetary data, highlighting objects with significantly different values.

```jsx
const solarSystem = [
  {
    name: 'Sun',
    orbitalDistanceKm: 0,
    radiusKm: 696340,
    massKg: 1.989e30,
    fill: '#ffa700',
  },
  {
    name: 'Mercury',
    orbitalDistanceKm: 5.791e7,
    radiusKm: 2439.7,
    massKg: 3.3e23,
    fill: '#1a1a1a',
  },
  // ... rest of the planets
]
```

--------------------------------

### Recharts Public API Import Example (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/DEVELOPING.md

Illustrates the correct method for importing components and types from the Recharts library, using its designated public API entry point. This adheres to the project's import restrictions, ensuring stability and preventing linter failures.

```typescript
import { TooltipIndex, DataKey, BarRectangleItem } from 'recharts';
```

--------------------------------

### Import XAxis Component and Stories in Storybook

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/cartesian/XAxis.mdx

Imports the Meta, Canvas, and Controls utilities from Storybook addon-docs for documentation, along with XAxis component stories. This setup enables interactive documentation and API control panels in Storybook.

```javascript
import { Meta, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as ComponentStories from './XAxis.stories';
```

--------------------------------

### Using Custom Recharts Components with BarChart (Recharts v3.0)

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/3.0-migration-guide

This example demonstrates how to integrate custom React components, such as `MyCustomAxes`, directly within a Recharts v3.0 chart. In previous versions (v2.0), this direct embedding was not supported, but v3.0 allows for greater flexibility by treating custom components as first-class citizens. This approach simplifies chart customization and component reuse.

```jsx
const MyCustomAxes = () => (
  <XAxis dataKey="name" />
  <YAxis tickCount={7} />
)

<BarChart width={1100} height={250} data={data}>
  <Bar dataKey="uv" />
  <MyCustomAxes />
</BarChart>
```

--------------------------------

### Display Component Properties with ArgTypes

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx

Renders an interactive ArgTypes table displaying all configurable properties of the RadarChart component, sorted with required properties first. This allows developers to explore and understand the component's API directly in the documentation.

```JavaScript
<ArgTypes of={RadarChartStories} sort={'requiredFirst'} />
```
